config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. In my view, society’s interests matter, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can be used to excuse abuse of power. A good society protects people even when it is inconvenient, especially minorities and those with less influence. In Indonesia, I have seen how quickly “for the common good” can become a justification for silencing ordinary people, so I am cautious about giving society priority over the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi hak individu harus menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilampaui, terutama untuk melindungi minoritas dan orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kalau hak pribadi bisa dikorbankan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu lebih praktis, maka hukum dan demokrasi jadi rentan disalahgunakan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu menang atas individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil tetap mengejar kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement: society’s interests should not automatically take priority over individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “for the public good” can be used to pressure people into silence, and that makes me wary of any system that treats the individual as secondary by default. Public order and social welfare matter, but they must be limited by clear rights, otherwise the majority or the state can easily justify unfair treatment of minorities and ordinary citizens. A healthy society is not one that always wins against the individual, but one that protects people even when that is inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikalahkan demi “kepentingan masyarakat.” Dari pengalaman nilai-nilai Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pengawasan berlebihan, diskriminasi, atau pengekangan kebebasan yang justru merugikan minoritas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi kalau masyarakat selalu diberi prioritas, maka orang yang paling lemah yang paling mudah dikorbankan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang bisa mengejar tujuan bersama tanpa menginjak hak dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “society first” can become a cover for silencing people and protecting those in power. Public interests matter, but they should be pursued within firm limits, otherwise minorities and ordinary citizens end up paying the price for decisions made in the name of the majority. A society is not truly strong if it only looks orderly on the surface while ignoring individual rights underneath. For me, the better principle is that society and the individual should be balanced, with rights acting as the boundary that public power cannot cross.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, terutama ketika negara atau mayoritas bisa memakai alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” untuk menekan orang yang lemah. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya sangat waspada terhadap pengawasan berlebihan, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan yang sering justru dibenarkan atas nama kepentingan publik. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dilanggar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling keras menuntut pengorbanan individu, melainkan yang mampu mengejar tujuan bersama tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
